tool.name: WGCNA: Soft-Thresholding Power
tool.description: Estimate the soft-thresholding power for WGCNA network construction

configfiles.configfile:
library(WGCNA)
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
counts_file = "$counts_file" network_type = "$network_type" corFnc = "$corFnc" log_transform = $log_transform filter_by_variance = $filter_by_variance variance_quantile = as.numeric($variance_quantile) RsquaredCut = as.numeric($RsquaredCut) softpower_plot = "plots/soft_thresholding.png" softpower_config = "softpower_config.txt" softpower_fit_indices = "softpower_fit_indices.tsv"
## Load count matrix counts <- read.delim( counts_file, check.names = FALSE, row.names = 1 )
## Prepare expression matrix expr_data <- as.matrix(counts) mode(expr_data) <- "numeric"
## Log-transform if requested if (log_transform) { expr_data <- log2(expr_data + 1) }
## Remove missing values if present if (any(is.na(expr_data))) { expr_data <- expr_data[complete.cases(expr_data), ] }
## Filter by variance if requested if (filter_by_variance) { gene_variances <- apply(expr_data, 1, var) variance_threshold <- quantile( gene_variances, variance_quantile, na.rm = TRUE ) filtered_expr_data <- expr_data[ gene_variances > variance_threshold, , drop = FALSE ] } else { filtered_expr_data <- expr_data }
datExpr <- t(filtered_expr_data)
## Enable multithreading when available try(enableWGCNAThreads(), silent = TRUE)
## Candidate powers powers <- c(seq(1, 10, by = 1), seq(12, 20, by = 2))
## Pick soft-thresholding power sft <- pickSoftThreshold( datExpr, powerVector = powers, RsquaredCut = RsquaredCut, networkType = network_type, corFnc = corFnc, verbose = 3 )
## Generate plot png(output_plot, width = 1200, height = 600, res = 100)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot( sft\$fitIndices[, 1], -sign(sft\$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft\$fitIndices[, 2], type = "n", xlab = "Soft Threshold (power)", ylab = "Scale Free Topology Model Fit, signed R^2", main = "Scale Independence" )
text( sft\$fitIndices[, 1], -sign(sft\$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft\$fitIndices[, 2], labels = sft\$fitIndices[, 1], cex = 0.9, col = "red" )
abline(h = RsquaredCut, col = "blue", lty = 2)
plot( sft\$fitIndices[, 1], sft\$fitIndices[, 5], type = "n", xlab = "Soft Threshold (power)", ylab = "Mean Connectivity", main = "Mean Connectivity" )
text( sft\$fitIndices[, 1], sft\$fitIndices[, 5], labels = sft\$fitIndices[, 1], cex = 0.9, col = "red" )
dev.off()
## Determine selected power soft_power <- sft\$powerEstimate ## then let user do r
## based on WGCNA FAQ https://github.com/edo98811/WGCNA_official_documentation/blob/main/faq.html get_soft_power <- function(n_samples, network_type = "signed") { network_type <- tolower(network_type)
if (network_type == "signed") { if (n_samples < 20) power <- 18 else if (n_samples <= 30) power <- 16 else if (n_samples <= 40) power <- 14 else power <- 12 } else { # unsigned or signed hybrid if (n_samples < 20) power <- 9 else if (n_samples <= 30) power <- 8 else if (n_samples <= 40) power <- 7 else power <- 6 } return(power) }
if (is.na(soft_power)) { soft_power <- get_soft_power(n_samples=nrow(datExpr),network_type=network_type) cat("No suitable power found. Using power based on WGCNA author's recommndation") } else { cat("Chosen soft-thresholding power:", soft_power, "\n") }
## Write configuration file writeLines( c( paste0("soft_power = ", soft_power), paste0("network_type = ", network_type), paste0("variance_quantile = ", variance_quantile) ), output_config )
## Write full fit indices table write.table( sft\$fitIndices, file = output_fit_indices, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE )
cat("Selected soft power:", soft_power, "\n") cat("Fit indices table written to:", output_fit_indices, "\n")

param.name: counts_file
param.label: Counts matrix
param.help: Genes as rows and samples as columns. First column contains gene identifiers.

param.name: RsquaredCut
param.label: Rsquared threshold to select the power
param.help: The lowest power for which the scale free topology fit exceeds this value will be selected

tool.help:
**WGCNA Soft-Thresholding Power Estimation**
Runs ``pickSoftThreshold`` from the WGCNA package to estimate an appropriate soft-thresholding power for weighted gene co-expression network construction.
**Inputs**
- **Counts matrix** TSV file with genes as rows and samples as columns.
- **Network type** Choice of unsigned (default), signed, or signed hybrid network.
- **Correlation function** Choice of Pearson correlation (default, cor) or biweight midcorrelation (bicor, more robust to outliers).
- **Log2-transform counts** Apply log2(count + 1) before network construction (recommended for RNA-seq data, enabled by default).
- **Filter low-variance genes** Remove low-variance genes before analysis (enabled by default). Low variance genes contribute less to the co-expression network and can be removed to improve computational efficiency.
- **Variance filter quantile** Genes below this variance percentile are removed (default 0.4, bottom 40%). Used only when filtering is enabled. This threshold is a reasonable default for typical gene expression datasets, removing genes that are not highly variable across samples.
**Outputs**
- **Soft-thresholding config** Configuration file containing the selected soft-thresholding power, network type, and variance quantile. If no power achieves an R-squared >= 0.85, the tool uses a default value of 6, which is a safe and commonly used default.
- **Soft-thresholding fit indices** Table with all fit indices calculated for each candidate power.
- **Soft-thresholding plot** Diagnostic plot showing scale-free topology fit (signed R-squared vs power) and mean connectivity vs power.
